LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT 3525 17TH STREET PARTNERS, LLC., STEVEN MAVROMIHALIS' MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF STATUTORY ATTORNEY'S FEES. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF STATUTORY ATTORNEYS' FEES IS DENIED. NEITHER PLAINTIFFS NOR DEFENDANTS PREVAILED ON A "PRACTICAL LEVEL" AND THUS, PER THE EXERCISE OF THE COURT'S DISCRETION, NEITHER SIDE IS THE PREVAILING PARTY FOR PURPOSES OF AN AWARD OF FEES PER SECTION 37.9(F) OF THE RENT ORDINANCE. (GILBERT V. NATIONAL ENQUIRER, INC. (1997) 55 CAL. APP. 4TH 1273, 1277-78). THE PARTIES' LEGAL RELATIONS PERTAINING TO THE OCCUPANCY OF THE UNITS FORMERLY OCCUPIED BY PLAINTIFFS AS A RESULT OF THE 2016 FIRE HAVE NOT BEEN FINALLY DETERMINED. AFTER THE FILING OF THIS LAWSUIT DEFENDANTS APPRISED PLAINTIFFS OF PERTINENT INFORMATION THAT HAD NOT BEEN PROVIDED TO THEM PRIOR TO THE LAWSUIT. PLAINTIFFS' CONTENTION THAT THEY WOULD NOT HAVE RECEIVED THIS INFORMATION BUT FOR THE FILING OF THE LAWSUIT APPEARS TO BE BOTH PLAUSIBLE AND COMPORTS WITH THE ALLEGATION IN PARAGRAPH 15 OF THE COMPLAINT THAT DEFENDANTS "STOPPED PROVIDING UPDATES TO" THE PLAINTIFFS. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR PLAINTIFF DAVID DOBSON'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANTS 3525 17TH STREET PARTNERS, LLC AND STEVEN MAVROMIHALIS MEMORANDUM OF COSTS; IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO TAX COSTS. (ADVANCED FROM 10/5/18 PER JUDGE KAHN) HEARING SET FOR SEP-27-2018 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, PLAINTIFF DAVID DOBSON'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANTS 3525 17TH STREET PARTNERS, LLC AND STEVEN MAVROMIHALIS MEMORANDUM OF COSTS; IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO TAX COSTS. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANTS' MEMORANDUM OF COSTS IS GRANTED. ALL COSTS CLAIMED BY DEFENDANTS IN THE COSTS MEMORANDUM FILED AUGUST 7, 2018 ARE STRICKEN. NEITHER PLAINTIFFS NOR DEFENDANTS PREVAILED ON A "PRACTICAL LEVEL" AND THUS, PER THE EXERCISE OF THE COURT'S DISCRETION, NEITHER SIDE IS THE PREVAILING PARTY FOR PURPOSES OF AN AWARD OF COSTS PER CCP 1032(A)(4). (GILBERT V. NATIONAL ENQUIRER, INC. (1997) 55 CAL. APP. 4TH 1273, 1277-78). THE PARTIES' LEGAL RELATIONS PERTAINING TO THE OCCUPANCY OF THE UNITS FORMERLY OCCUPIED BY PLAINTIFFS AS A RESULT OF THE 2016 FIRE HAVE NOT BEEN FINALLY DETERMINED. AFTER THE FILING OF THIS LAWSUIT DEFENDANTS APPRISED PLAINTIFFS OF PERTINENT INFORMATION THAT HAD NOT BEEN PROVIDED TO THEM PRIOR TO THE LAWSUIT. PLAINTIFFS' CONTENTION THAT THEY WOULD NOT HAVE RECEIVED THIS INFORMATION BUT FOR THE FILING OF THE LAWSUIT APPEARS TO BE BOTH PLAUSIBLE AND COMPORTS WITH THE ALLEGATION IN PARAGRAPH 15 OF THE COMPLAINT THAT DEFENDANTS "STOPPED PROVIDING UPDATES TO" THE PLAINTIFFS. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

calendar.matters:
Plaintiff David Dobson'S Motion To Strike Defendants 3525 17th Street Partners, Llc And Steven Mavromihalis Memorandum Of Costs; In The Alternative, To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Oct-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff David Dobson'S Motion To Strike Defendants 3525 17th Street Partners, Llc And Steven Mavromihalis Memorandum Of Costs; In The Alternative, To Tax Costs is OFF CALENDAR. Advanced to September 27, 2018 and was heard by Judge Kahn. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600

Plaintiff David Dobson'S Motion To Strike Defendants 3525 17th Street Partners, Llc And Steven Mavromihalis Memorandum Of Costs; In The Alternative, To Tax Costs. (Advanced From 10/5/18 Per Judge Kahn)
Off Calendar Sep-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Schreiber, Esq. 415-835-6777 of Bracamontes & Vlasak for Plaintiff David Dobson.
William Weisberg, Esq. 415-296-7070 of Weisberg & Miller for Defendant 3525 17TH STREET PARTNERS, LLC., STEVEN MAVROMIHALIS.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff David Dobson'S Motion To Strike Defendants 3525 17th Street Partners, Llc And Steven Mavromihalis Memorandum Of Costs; In The Alternative, To Tax Costs.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiffs' motion to strike defendants' memorandum of costs is granted. All costs claimed by defendants in the costs memorandum filed August 7, 2018 are stricken. Neither plaintiffs nor defendants prevailed on a "practical level" and thus, per the exercise of the court's discretion, neither side is the prevailing party for purposes of an award of costs per CCP 1032(a)(4). (Gilbert v. National Enquirer, Inc. (1997) 55 Cal. App. 4th 1273, 1277-78). The parties' legal relations pertaining to the occupancy of the units formerly occupied by plaintiffs as a result of the 2016 fire have not been finally determined. After the filing of this lawsuit defendants apprised plaintiffs of pertinent information that had not been provided to them prior to the lawsuit. Plaintiffs' contention that they would not have received this information but for the filing of the lawsuit appears to be both plausible and comports with the allegation in paragraph 15 of the complaint that defendants "stopped providing updates to" the plaintiffs.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Sep-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Schreiber, Esq. 415-835-6777 of Bracamontes & Vlasak for Plaintiff David Dobson.
William Weisberg, Esq. 415-296-7070 of Weisberg & Miller for Defendant 3525 17TH STREET PARTNERS, LLC., STEVEN MAVROMIHALIS.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT 3525 17TH STREET PARTNERS, LLC., STEVEN MAVROMIHALIS' MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF STATUTORY ATTORNEY'S FEES.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants' motion for an award of statutory attorneys' fees is denied. Neither plaintiffs nor defendants prevailed on a "practical level" and thus, per the exercise of the court's discretion, neither side is the prevailing party for purposes of an award of fees per section 37.9(f) of the Rent Ordinance. (Gilbert v. National Enquirer, Inc. (1997) 55 Cal. App. 4th 1273, 1277-78). The parties' legal relations pertaining to the occupancy of the units formerly occupied by plaintiffs as a result of the 2016 fire have not been finally determined. After the filing of this lawsuit defendants apprised plaintiffs of pertinent information that had not been provided to them prior to the lawsuit. Plaintiffs' contention that they would not have received this information but for the filing of the lawsuit appears to be both plausible and comports with the allegation in paragraph 15 of the complaint that defendants "stopped providing updates to" the plaintiffs.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
